privileged_info_summary: The cranioplasty_implant_pipeline task uses the CT Cranium 0051 dataset from the InVesalius GitHub repository (v3.0 release). The dataset contains 108 CT axial slices of a male cranium scanned on 2002-06-04. Critical dimensional discrepancies exist in the task documentation: the actual in-plane pixel spacing is 0.4785 mm (NOT 0.957 mm as claimed in fab_specs.txt), the slice thickness is 2.0 mm (NOT 1.5 mm as claimed), and the maximum Hounsfield unit value is 3033 HU (NOT 3071 HU as claimed). An internal contradiction exists in the metadata: task.json specifies compact_bone_hu_min of 600 HU while fab_specs.txt specifies 662 HU. The actual task workflow requires: (1) creating 3 masks (full bone 226-3071 HU, compact bone with conflicting specifications, and a boolean MINUS result), (2) generating 3D surfaces from both compact and cancellous bone masks, (3) optimizing the compact bone surface via smoothing (≥10 iterations) and decimation (<400,000 triangles), (4) exporting in PLY format (cortical) and STL format (cancellous), and (5) placing ≥5 calvarial measurements ≥10 mm in length. The verifier checks these criteria with a pass threshold of 65/100 points.
